breakpoints.name: Heavy armour + domain dip
breakpoints.effect: Proficiency in heavy armour, shields and martial weapons, domain spells, and (War) a bonus-action War Priest attack. Cleric uses Wisdom, so a 1-level dip costs no extra ability investment.
breakpoints.combos: The standard opener for Strength throwers and any martial that wants free heavy armour. Tempest sets up Destructive Wrath; Life sets up the buff-on-heal engine.

breakpoints.name: Charisma weapon attacks + short-rest slots
breakpoints.effect: Pact of the Blade binds any weapon to use Charisma for attack AND damage, and warlock spell slots recharge on a SHORT rest — so you cast (and smite) far more often than a long-rest class.
breakpoints.combos: A single Hexblade level turns any Charisma build into a weapon user. It is the keystone of the Lockadin (smite on short-rest slots) and pairs with Sorcerer or Bard for a fully Charisma-based character.

breakpoints.name: Fighting Style + Second Wind
breakpoints.effect: A Fighting Style (Archery = +2 to ranged hit, Defence = +1 AC, Dueling, etc.), Second Wind self-heal, and Strength & Constitution save proficiency if Fighter is your first level.
breakpoints.combos: A 1-level dip gives archers the Archery style and throwers a durable base. Most martial multiclasses pass through here.

breakpoints.name: First-level Con saves + casting
breakpoints.effect: If taken as your FIRST level, Sorcerer grants Constitution save proficiency — the single best way to protect concentration on a caster. Full Charisma spellcasting follows.
breakpoints.combos: The classic 'take one level first' pick for almost every caster multiclass: Lightning Lord, tank wizard, and sorlocks all open on Sorcerer for the Con save.

breakpoints.name: Cunning Action
breakpoints.effect: Bonus-action Dash, Disengage or Hide every turn — enormous mobility and safety for ranged and melee skirmishers.
breakpoints.combos: A cheap 2-level dip that keeps archers kiting and lets melee re-hide for sneak attacks.

breakpoints.name: Combat Wild Shape + Spike Growth
breakpoints.effect: Wild Shape as a BONUS action into combat-ready beasts (extra HP bars), plus access to Spike Growth — a game-winning control spell — from level 2.
breakpoints.combos: The best early-game carry in the game. Forms keep scaling, so Moon Druid is strong from level 2 to 12 with no contested gear.

breakpoints.name: Starry Form
breakpoints.effect: Bonus-action Starry Form: Archer (a free ranged radiant bonus-action attack), Chalice (bonus-action heals) or Dragon (rock-solid concentration). Guidance and Bardic-style dice on tap.
breakpoints.combos: A 2-level Stars dip gives any Wisdom caster a reliable bonus-action ranged attack while they buff or control — great on a support cleric/druid.

breakpoints.name: Action Surge
breakpoints.effect: Once per short rest, take an ENTIRE extra action — a second round of attacks, throws, or Scorching Rays in a single turn.
breakpoints.combos: The burst engine for nearly every build in the game: martials double their swings and casters fire two big spells back-to-back.

breakpoints.name: Destructive Wrath
breakpoints.effect: Channel Divinity to MAXIMISE the damage of one lightning or thunder roll — no dice, just the highest possible number.
breakpoints.combos: The core of the Lightning Lord one-shot: maximise a big Witch Bolt or Chromatic Orb on a Wet target for guaranteed enormous damage.

breakpoints.name: Divine Smite + Fighting Style
breakpoints.effect: Spend any spell slot on hit for a burst of radiant damage (more on a higher slot, even more vs Fiends/Undead), plus a Fighting Style.
breakpoints.combos: Pair with Hexblade/warlock short-rest slots and you smite many more times per day than a pure paladin — the Lockadin.

breakpoints.name: Arcane Ward
breakpoints.effect: A damage-soaking ward that recharges whenever you cast an abjuration spell — it absorbs huge flat damage and makes you nearly unkillable.
breakpoints.combos: Glyph of Warding tops the ward back up for free every turn; add a Sorcerer dip for Armor of Agathys retaliation.

breakpoints.name: Slashing Flourish
breakpoints.effect: Spend a Bardic die to make one attack hit a SECOND target, or to roughly DOUBLE a single ranged attack's damage — on top of full bard spellcasting and the best skills.
breakpoints.combos: The machine-gun archer engine: every arrow can be Flourished. Stacks with Extra Attack at Bard 6.

breakpoints.name: Second bonus action
breakpoints.effect: A SECOND bonus action every single turn — double your Flurries, off-hand attacks, or thrown weapons.
breakpoints.combos: A force-multiplier on monks (more Flurry of Blows), throwers (two bonus-action throws), and dual-wielders.

breakpoints.name: Surprise auto-crit + restored action
breakpoints.effect: Automatic critical hits against surprised enemies, and your action AND bonus action are refreshed at the start of your first turn in combat.
breakpoints.combos: The alpha-strike archer: stack with Gloomstalker and Action Surge to delete the most dangerous enemy before it acts.

breakpoints.name: Frenzy bonus-action attack
breakpoints.effect: While raging, make a bonus-action attack — or THROW — every turn, riding your rage damage bonus.
breakpoints.combos: With Thief's second bonus action that's two extra throws a turn, each able to knock prone — the Boo-throwing barbarian.

breakpoints.name: Dread Ambusher
breakpoints.effect: An extra attack and bonus movement on the FIRST turn of every combat, plus invisibility in darkness and +Wisdom initiative.
breakpoints.combos: Stacks with Assassin auto-crits and Action Surge for a devastating surprise round; the backbone of the burst archer.

breakpoints.name: Crowd control on Flurry
breakpoints.effect: Flurry of Blows can knock prone, push away, or block reactions — free hard control attached to your main attack.
breakpoints.combos: Turns the monk's flood of attacks into a flood of control; pairs with Thief for even more Flurries.

breakpoints.name: Bound weapon + Shield spell
breakpoints.effect: Bind a thrown weapon so it always returns to your hand, and learn the Shield reaction (+5 AC) and Longstrider — huge survivability for a martial.
breakpoints.combos: The durable spine of the Eldritch Knight thrower: never lose your weapon, almost never get hit.

breakpoints.name: Extra Attack
breakpoints.effect: A second attack on every Attack action. This roughly DOUBLES weapon throughput and is the single biggest martial power spike in the game.
breakpoints.combos: Almost every martial wants to reach class level 5 before multiclassing. Below 5, weapon builds feel weak; at 5 they come alive.

breakpoints.name: Stunning Strike + Extra Attack
breakpoints.effect: Spend ki to STUN an enemy (against your save DC) and gain Extra Attack. A stunned enemy loses its turn and is easy to crit.
breakpoints.combos: With Tavern Brawler your DC is high enough to stun-lock most encounters — fights often end before the enemy acts.

breakpoints.name: Elemental Affinity
breakpoints.effect: Add your Charisma modifier to EVERY instance of your bloodline's element — e.g. each of the seven beams of an upcast Scorching Ray.
breakpoints.combos: The moment the Fire Sorcerer comes online: apply your maxed Charisma seven-plus times a turn, then Twin or Quicken a second spell.

breakpoints.name: Extra Attack (Flourished)
breakpoints.effect: Extra Attack stacks with Slashing Flourish: two attacks, each Flourished, can hit up to four times a turn — all on full bard casting.
breakpoints.combos: The Swords Bard machine-gun fully online; add Action Surge from a Fighter dip for a fifth and sixth Flourished shot.

breakpoints.name: Improved Extra Attack (triple attack)
breakpoints.effect: THREE attacks per Attack action — the only class that reaches it. With Action Surge that's six, plus a War Cleric bonus attack makes up to seven in the opening round.
breakpoints.combos: The end-game ceiling for throwers and martial archers: Fighter 11 / War Cleric 1 is the most attacks-per-round split in the game.

breakpoints.name: Peak Wild Shape forms
breakpoints.effect: Access to the strongest forms — multiattack beasts and, via Tavern Brawler, Myrmidons that swing multiple times a turn — backed by endless HP bars and the best summons.
breakpoints.combos: The shapeshifter capstone: a near-unkillable melee form plus Myrmidon/Woodland Being summons doing a second character's worth of damage.

playstyles.label: Throw weapons at people
playstyles.engine: Tavern Brawler + a returning thrown weapon + a Strength elixir. Every throw applies the weapon's on-hit effects and can knock the target prone.

recs.why: Open on Cleric for heavy armour, shields, and a bonus-action War Priest attack. Grab a returning weapon and start throwing.

recs.split: Fighter 3 / War Cleric 1 → Tavern Brawler at Fighter 4
recs.why: Once Tavern Brawler lands (next level) your throws stop missing — the whole build hinges on this feat.
Two throws per action, four with Action Surge, plus the cleric bonus throw.
Bound weapon + Shield spell make you nearly unkillable while you close in on triple-throw.

recs.split: Eldritch Knight 11 / War Cleric 1  (or Berserker 5 / Thief 4 / Fighter 3)
recs.why: Improved Extra Attack — up to seven near-guaranteed throws in the opening round. The most forgiving way to win Honour Mode.

playstyles.label: Rain arrows like a machine gun
playstyles.engine: Sharpshooter + the Archery style, then Swords Bard Flourishes that double every shot — or a Gloomstalker/Assassin alpha strike.

recs.why: Pick up a bow and the +2-to-hit Archery style; bank Sharpshooter for your first feat.
Every shot can now double its damage or hit a second target, on top of bard casting and the best skills.
Two Flourished attacks = up to four hits a turn.
Dread Ambusher + Assassin auto-crits + Action Surge delete the scariest enemy before it acts — the safe, bursty alternative.
Action Surge + the Arcane Acuity / Mystic Scoundrel combo: out-damages dedicated archers AND lands any control spell. The #1 Honour build.

playstyles.label: Punch everything to death
playstyles.engine: Open Hand Monk + Tavern Brawler + Thief's second bonus action — a flood of near-guaranteed unarmed strikes with free prone and stun.

recs.why: Start high Dex/Wis; your Strength comes from a daily elixir later. Martial Arts + Unarmored Defence.
Flurry of Blows stops missing once Tavern Brawler is in and you start drinking a Strength elixir.
Stun-lock most encounters; a stunned enemy loses its turn and eats crits.
A second bonus action means up to 8–10 attacks a turn of Flurry, all with free prone/push from Open Hand.
Peak sustained damage and control — the best melee character in the game; most fights end before the enemy acts.

playstyles.label: Smite things with a glowing sword
playstyles.engine: Charisma weapon attacks via Hexblade (Pact of the Blade) plus short-rest warlock slots burned on Divine Smite — everything keyed to one stat.

recs.why: Charisma weapon attacks and short-rest spell slots — the keystone that makes the whole build single-stat.
Burn your (often short-rest) slots for radiant bursts on hit.
Two smiting swings a turn, both on Charisma.
Smite far more often than a pure paladin, plus Eldritch Blast, Hunger of Hadar, Darkness abuse and high-DC Command — burst, control and face skills in one.

playstyles.label: Burn the whole battlefield
playstyles.engine: Draconic (Red) Sorcerer — Elemental Affinity adds Charisma to every fire hit, and an upcast Scorching Ray fires up to seven beams.

recs.why: Start 17 Charisma; take Shield + Mage Armour. Con saves protect your concentration.
Quicken a second spell or Twin a buff/nuke onto two targets.
Your main damage spell for the rest of the game.
+Charisma to every fire instance. The build comes fully online — seven beams each carrying your maxed Charisma.
Sixth-level slots = a seven-beam Scorching Ray; Twin or Quicken a second one. Almost nothing survives.

playstyles.label: One-shot a boss from across the map
playstyles.engine: Make the target Wet, MAXIMISE one huge lightning/thunder spell with Tempest Cleric's Destructive Wrath, and guarantee the hit with Divination portent dice.

recs.why: Open for Con saves so your big concentration turn can't be interrupted.
Destructive Wrath maximises a lightning or thunder roll — no dice, just the ceiling.
Quicken Create Water for Wet (double lightning), then drop a maximised Chromatic Orb.
Portent dice guarantee the hit; a maxed, Wet-doubled 6th-level spell does ~300 damage to one target on turn one.

playstyles.label: Be literally unkillable
playstyles.engine: Abjuration Arcane Ward (refilled for free by Glyph of Warding) plus Armor of Agathys cold retaliation from a White Dragon Sorcerer dip.

recs.why: Con saves plus Armor of Agathys — ~60 cold retaliation (doubled vs Wet enemies) whenever you're hit.
Arcane Ward soaks large flat damage and refreshes on every abjuration spell.
A full wizard that simply cannot die — Glyph of Warding tops the ward every turn while you control the field.

playstyles.label: Shapeshift and summon a zoo
playstyles.engine: Circle of the Moon bonus-action Wild Shape (multiple HP bars) plus the best summons in the game — all on Wisdom, no contested gear.

recs.why: Wisdom-only; you'll never fight over loot with the rest of the party.
Bonus-action combat Wild Shape plus Spike Growth — the best early-game carry, strong from the moment you get it.
Makes your melee Wild Shape forms hit hard and reliably.
Peak multiattack forms (Myrmidons via Tavern Brawler) plus Myrmidon/Woodland Being summons — endless HP and a second character's worth of damage.

playstyles.label: Buff the party & stack radiant orbs
playstyles.engine: Twelve levels of Cleric — Spirit Guardians + Luminous Armour for radiant-orb / reverberation stacking, plus the buff-on-heal item engine.

recs.why: Heavy armour and your domain online from turn one.
Starry Form Archer gives a free bonus-action ranged attack while you spend your action buffing or controlling.
The radiant-orb / reverberation engine: enemies lose accuracy and get knocked down just for standing near you.
Guidance, Aid, Heroes' Feast, plus (Light) Warding Flare to be nearly unhittable. Fills any role; only weakness is skills.
